summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View column about tools and efforts to combat problematic algorithms. The post itself contains no substantive analysis, serving purely as a pointer to external content. O'Neil uses her characteristic framing of algorithms as 'overlords' to signal the adversarial relationship between automated systems and the public interest.
key_points: Announces a new Bloomberg View column on fighting bad algorithms
Frames algorithms as 'overlords' suggesting power imbalance and need for resistance
Directs readers to her archive of Bloomberg columns for more content
money_quotes: Look Who's Fighting Our Algorithmic Overlords
key_insight: The post serves as a gateway to O'Neil's journalism on algorithmic accountability rather than offering standalone analysis.
